prerequisites.note: Frontend pages do not exist yet — the web app (apps/web) is a stub. This test plan is a specification for E2E tests once the frontend is built (expected in E6+ epics). Tests assume a running API at :3000, a Vite dev server at :5173, and a seeded database with test users.

admin.description: Primary admin user with full access — used for most journeys

sales_staff.description: Limited user — cannot access admin pages

journeys.name: Admin Login and Permission-Driven Sidebar
journeys.description: Verify that an ADMIN user with FULL_ACCESS group sees all System module sidebar items after login. The sidebar is built from GET /system/my-permissions.

steps.expected_result: Login page loads with email and password fields
steps.visual_check: Login form visible with email input, password input, and Sign In button

steps.expected_result: Form fields populated
Login succeeds, redirected to dashboard
steps.visual_check: Dashboard loaded with app shell — sidebar visible with System module expanded showing: Users, Company Profile, Resource Registry, Access Groups

steps.expected_result: System module section contains links for Users, Company Profile, Resource Registry, and Access Groups
All four System module sidebar items visible for FULL_ACCESS user

journeys.name: View System Resource Registry
journeys.description: Admin navigates to the Resource Registry page and verifies all seeded resources are displayed with correct metadata. This is a read-only T1 Entity List page.

steps.expected_result: Navigates to /system/resources
Resource Registry heading visible
steps.visual_check: T1 Entity List page with Resource Registry title, no [+ New] button (read-only), table listing resources with columns: Code, Name, Module, Type, Sort Order

steps.expected_result: Table contains at least 6 rows for seeded system resources
All 6 seeded system resource codes visible in the table
Table filtered to show only system module resources
steps.visual_check: Filter dropdown showing 'system' selected, table showing only system-module resources

steps.expected_result: Table filtered to show only PAGE type resources
Table filtered to show resources matching 'access' in code, name, or description
steps.visual_check: Search box shows 'access', table filtered to show access-groups.list and access-groups.detail resources

journeys.name: View Access Group List with Pre-Built Groups
journeys.description: Admin navigates to Access Groups page and verifies all 12 pre-built system groups are listed with correct metadata. T1 Entity List with cursor pagination.

steps.expected_result: Navigates to /system/access-groups
Access Groups heading visible
steps.visual_check: T1 Entity List page with Access Groups title, [+ New Access Group] button visible, table listing groups with columns: Code, Name, Description, System badge, Active Users, Created

steps.expected_result: Table contains 12 rows for pre-built system groups
All 12 pre-built access group codes visible
System badge/indicator visible on all 12 pre-built groups
Table filtered to show groups containing 'manager'
steps.visual_check: Search showing 'manager', table showing FINANCE_MANAGER, SALES_MANAGER, PURCHASE_MANAGER, HR_MANAGER

steps.expected_result: Search cleared, all groups visible again

journeys.name: Create a Custom Access Group
journeys.description: Admin creates a new custom access group with a unique code, name, and description via the create form. Verifies it appears in the list.

steps.expected_result: Access group list page loaded
Navigates to /system/access-groups/new — create form displayed
steps.visual_check: T7 Settings template in create mode with empty form fields: Code (text input), Name (text input), Description (textarea)

input_data.name: QA Tester
input_data.description: Custom access group for QA testing E2E flows

steps.expected_result: Form fields populated with custom group data
Access group created successfully — 201 response
steps.visual_check: Success toast 'Access group created', redirected to detail page for QA_TESTER showing code (read-only after creation), name, description, empty permission matrix

steps.expected_result: Access group list now shows 13 groups (12 pre-built + QA_TESTER)
steps.visual_check: Access group list with QA_TESTER row visible, no System badge on QA_TESTER (isSystem: false), user count showing 0

journeys.name: Create Access Group with Duplicate Code is Rejected
journeys.description: Verify that creating an access group with an existing code for the same company returns a 409 Conflict error.

steps.expected_result: Create form loaded
Form populated
409 Conflict error displayed — code already exists
steps.visual_check: Error toast or inline error showing 'Access group code already exists for this company', form remains on create page

journeys.name: View Access Group Detail with Permission Matrix
journeys.description: Admin views a pre-built system group (FULL_ACCESS) detail page showing the full permission matrix and field overrides panel. T7 Settings template.

steps.expected_result: Access group list loaded
Navigates to /system/access-groups/:fullAccessId — detail page loaded
steps.visual_check: T7 Settings page with breadcrumb 'System > Access Groups > Full Access', title 'Full Access', Active status badge, [Save Settings] button, system group banner visible

steps.expected_result: Code (read-only for system groups) and name displayed
Checkbox grid visible with resources grouped by module (System collapsible section), columns: canAccess, canNew, canView, canEdit, canDelete
steps.visual_check: Permission matrix showing System module expanded with all 6 resources, each row has 5 checkboxes, FULL_ACCESS has all checkboxes checked (all true)

steps.target: System module collapsible section in permission matrix
steps.expected_result: System section shows: User Management, User Detail, Company Profile, Resource Registry, Access Group List, Access Group Detail — all with all 5 checkboxes checked
Field overrides panel visible (empty for FULL_ACCESS — no overrides set)
steps.visual_check: Field Overrides section visible with resource selector dropdown, no overrides configured for FULL_ACCESS group

journeys.name: Edit Access Group Name and Description
journeys.description: Admin edits the name and description of the custom QA_TESTER group. Also tests that system groups can have name/description modified.

steps.expected_result: Access group list loaded
QA_TESTER detail page loaded

input_data.name: QA Testing Team
input_data.description: Updated description for the QA testing access group

steps.expected_result: Name and description fields updated
Metadata updated successfully — 200 response
steps.visual_check: Success toast 'Access group updated', page shows updated name 'QA Testing Team' and new description

steps.expected_result: List shows updated name 'QA Testing Team' for the QA_TESTER group

journeys.name: Configure Permission Matrix for Custom Group
journeys.description: Admin configures the permission matrix for QA_TESTER group — enabling specific canAccess/canView permissions on some resources and saving. Verifies replace-all semantics.

steps.expected_result: Access group list loaded
QA_TESTER detail page loaded with empty permission matrix (all unchecked)
steps.visual_check: Permission matrix with all 6 resources visible, all checkboxes unchecked (new group has no permissions)

steps.expected_result: canAccess checkbox now checked for Users List
canView checkbox now checked for Users List
canAccess checkbox now checked for Users Detail
canView checkbox now checked for Users Detail
canAccess checkbox now checked for Company Profile
canView checkbox now checked for Company Profile
steps.visual_check: Permission matrix showing users.list (canAccess+canView), users.detail (canAccess+canView), company-profile (canAccess+canView) checked; resources.list and access-groups remain unchecked

steps.expected_result: Permissions saved — PUT /access-groups/:id/permissions returns 200
steps.visual_check: Success toast 'Permissions updated', permission matrix retains the checked state

steps.expected_result: Return to list
Detail page reloaded — permissions persisted correctly
steps.visual_check: Permission matrix shows the same 6 checkboxes still checked from previous save — data persisted via GET detail API

journeys.name: Configure Field-Level Visibility Overrides
journeys.description: Admin configures field overrides on the QA_TESTER group — setting a field to HIDDEN and another to READ_ONLY on a resource. Verifies the field override panel.

steps.expected_result: Access group list loaded
QA_TESTER detail page loaded
Field overrides panel visible with resource selector
steps.visual_check: Field Overrides panel with 'Select Resource' dropdown, no overrides configured yet

steps.expected_result: Resource selected, field override table shows available fields for company-profile
vatNumber field set to HIDDEN
registrationNumber field set to READ_ONLY
steps.visual_check: Field override table showing vatNumber = HIDDEN, registrationNumber = READ_ONLY with dropdown selectors

steps.expected_result: Field overrides saved via PUT /access-groups/:id/field-overrides — 200
steps.visual_check: Success toast 'Field overrides updated', override table retains HIDDEN and READ_ONLY settings

journeys.name: Assign Access Groups to a User
journeys.description: Admin navigates to a user's detail page and assigns the QA_TESTER custom group alongside an existing system group. Verifies the replace-all assignment pattern.

steps.expected_result: User list page loaded
User detail page loaded for the sales staff user
steps.visual_check: T2 Record Detail page for the sales user, showing user info and Access Groups assignment panel with current groups (SALES_STAFF)

steps.expected_result: Panel shows currently assigned groups — SALES_STAFF displayed as tag/chip
Dropdown or modal showing available access groups for assignment
Both SALES_STAFF and QA_TESTER selected
Assignments saved via PUT /system/users/:id/access-groups — 200
steps.visual_check: Success toast 'Access groups updated', panel shows both SALES_STAFF and QA_TESTER as assigned groups with assignedBy and assignedAt timestamps

steps.expected_result: Return to user list
User detail reloaded — assignments persisted
steps.visual_check: Access Groups panel shows both SALES_STAFF and QA_TESTER groups assigned, verifying persistence

journeys.name: Assign Zero Access Groups Rejected (422)
journeys.description: Verify that attempting to assign zero access groups to a user returns a 422 Business Rule Violation — at least one group is required.

steps.expected_result: User list loaded
User detail page loaded
All access groups deselected — empty selection
422 error — at least one access group is required
steps.visual_check: Error toast or inline error showing 'At least one access group is required', assignments not saved, previous groups remain

journeys.name: Verify Permission Enforcement - Limited User Denied Access
journeys.description: Login as the sales_staff user (SALES_STAFF group) and verify that admin-only pages like Resource Registry and Access Groups are NOT visible in the sidebar and return 403 if navigated to directly.

steps.expected_result: Login page loaded (may need to logout first)
Form populated
Login succeeds, dashboard loaded
steps.visual_check: Dashboard loaded, sidebar does NOT show Resource Registry or Access Groups links (SALES_STAFF lacks canAccess on system.resources.list and system.access-groups.list). Only Company Profile and Users visible if SALES_STAFF+QA_TESTER grants that access.

steps.expected_result: Resource Registry and Access Groups NOT visible in sidebar
403 Forbidden or redirect to unauthorized page
steps.visual_check: Access denied message displayed — user does not have canAccess permission on system.resources.list

steps.expected_result: 403 Forbidden or redirect to unauthorized page
steps.visual_check: Access denied message displayed — user does not have canAccess permission on system.access-groups.list

journeys.name: Verify Most-Permissive-Wins Permission Resolution
journeys.description: The sales user has both SALES_STAFF (limited access) and QA_TESTER (users.list canAccess+canView). Verify that the union of permissions is applied — user can view the Users page via QA_TESTER even though SALES_STAFF doesn't grant it.

steps.expected_result: Login page loaded
Login form populated
Login succeeds
Users link visible in sidebar — granted by QA_TESTER's canAccess on system.users.list
User list page loads successfully (not 403)
steps.visual_check: User list page visible showing users — most-permissive-wins resolved canAccess=true from QA_TESTER group even though SALES_STAFF doesn't grant it

steps.expected_result: [+ New User] button should be HIDDEN — QA_TESTER only has canAccess+canView, not canNew
steps.visual_check: User list page with no [+ New User] button visible — canNew is false for system.users.list in QA_TESTER group

journeys.name: Verify Field-Level Visibility in API Response
journeys.description: The sales user (with QA_TESTER group having HIDDEN vatNumber and READ_ONLY registrationNumber on company-profile) views the company profile. HIDDEN fields should be absent, READ_ONLY fields should have _fieldMeta annotation.

steps.expected_result: Company profile page loaded (QA_TESTER grants canAccess+canView on company-profile)
steps.visual_check: Company profile page loaded — vatNumber field should be ABSENT (HIDDEN), registrationNumber field should be present but marked as read-only

steps.expected_result: vatNumber field is not displayed (HIDDEN by QA_TESTER field override)
registrationNumber field is present but displayed as read-only (READ_ONLY override)
steps.visual_check: registrationNumber field visible but non-editable (greyed out, disabled, or with read-only indicator), reflecting the READ_ONLY field override from QA_TESTER group

journeys.name: SUPER_ADMIN Bypasses All Permission Checks
journeys.description: Login as SUPER_ADMIN user and verify full access to all pages, all fields visible (no field filtering), and no access group assignment required.

steps.expected_result: Login page loaded
Form populated
Login succeeds, dashboard loaded with full sidebar
steps.visual_check: Dashboard with full sidebar showing all System module items (Users, Company Profile, Resource Registry, Access Groups) — SUPER_ADMIN bypass means all resources accessible

steps.expected_result: Resource Registry accessible — SUPER_ADMIN bypass
Access Groups accessible — SUPER_ADMIN bypass
Company profile page loaded with ALL fields visible — SUPER_ADMIN skips field filtering
steps.visual_check: Company profile shows ALL fields including vatNumber and registrationNumber fully editable — no _fieldMeta applied, SUPER_ADMIN bypass means no field filtering

journeys.name: Verify My Permissions Endpoint Data
journeys.description: Call GET /system/my-permissions for different users and verify the response drives the UI correctly — permissions, accessGroups, enabledModules, and isSuperAdmin flag.

steps.expected_result: Login page loaded
Form populated
Login succeeds
My permissions page or developer info panel loads showing resolved permissions
steps.visual_check: Permissions view showing: role (ADMIN), isSuperAdmin (false), accessGroups list including FULL_ACCESS, permissions object with all system resources having all flags true, enabledModules including 'system'

steps.expected_result: Role, access group, and enabled module displayed correctly

journeys.name: System Access Group Cannot Be Deleted
journeys.description: Admin attempts to delete/deactivate a system group (isSystem: true) and verifies the operation is rejected with 409 Conflict.

steps.expected_result: Access group list loaded (logged in as admin)
READ_ONLY detail page loaded — system group banner visible
steps.visual_check: T7 Settings page for READ_ONLY group with system group banner 'This is a system access group — code cannot be changed and it cannot be deleted'

steps.expected_result: Overflow menu opens
Deactivate option is disabled or has tooltip explaining system groups cannot be deleted
steps.visual_check: Overflow menu showing Deactivate option disabled/greyed out with tooltip 'System access groups cannot be deactivated'

journeys.name: Soft-Delete Custom Access Group
journeys.description: Admin deactivates the QA_TESTER custom group (after removing user assignments). Verifies soft-delete behavior — isActive becomes false.

steps.expected_result: User list loaded (admin session)
Sales user detail page loaded
Only SALES_STAFF selected — QA_TESTER removed
Assignment saved — sales user now only has SALES_STAFF
steps.visual_check: Success toast, Access Groups panel shows only SALES_STAFF for the sales user

steps.expected_result: Access group list loaded
QA_TESTER detail page loaded — user count now 0
Overflow menu opens
Confirmation dialog appears
steps.visual_check: Confirmation dialog: 'Are you sure you want to deactivate QA Testing Team? This will remove the group from active use.'

steps.expected_result: Group deactivated — 204 response from DELETE endpoint
steps.visual_check: Success toast 'Access group deactivated', redirected to access group list, QA_TESTER no longer visible in active group list (isActive filter defaults to true)

journeys.name: Read-Only User Cannot Create, Edit, or Delete
journeys.description: Login as the read_only user (READ_ONLY group) and verify they can view pages but cannot create, edit, or delete — buttons are hidden and direct actions are blocked.

steps.expected_result: Login page loaded
Form populated
Login succeeds, dashboard loaded
steps.visual_check: Dashboard loaded with sidebar showing system module items — READ_ONLY group has canAccess+canView on all resources

steps.expected_result: User list page accessible (canAccess+canView is true)
steps.visual_check: User list visible but [+ New User] button is HIDDEN — canNew is false for READ_ONLY group

steps.expected_result: User detail page loads (canView is true)
steps.visual_check: User detail page visible in read-only mode — all form fields are disabled/read-only, no Edit or Delete buttons visible (canEdit and canDelete are false)

steps.expected_result: Access group list accessible (canAccess+canView is true for READ_ONLY)
steps.visual_check: Access group list visible but [+ New Access Group] button is HIDDEN — canNew is false for READ_ONLY group

journeys.name: Export and Import Permission Configuration
journeys.description: Admin exports the company's permission configuration as JSON and re-imports it (dry run first, then actual import). Verifies round-trip fidelity.

steps.expected_result: Company profile page loaded (admin session)
Overflow menu opens with Export Config and Import Config options
steps.visual_check: Overflow menu showing 'Export Config' and 'Import Config' options

steps.expected_result: GET /system/company-profile/export-defaults called — JSON download initiated or preview shown
steps.visual_check: Export dialog or JSON preview showing version, exportedAt, exportedFrom (company name), resources array, accessGroups array with permissions and fieldOverrides, vatCodes, paymentTerms, numberSeries, currencies

steps.expected_result: Export JSON contains all expected sections
Import dialog opens with JSON input area and dry-run toggle
steps.visual_check: Import dialog with JSON textarea/file upload area, dry-run toggle checkbox, and Import button

steps.expected_result: Dry-run mode enabled, JSON pasted/loaded
Dry run executed — POST /system/company-profile/import-defaults with dryRun:true returns DRY_RUN status with summary counts
steps.visual_check: Import result showing status: DRY_RUN, summary with counts (resources created/updated, access groups created/updated, permissions set), no warnings — dry run validated without persisting

steps.expected_result: Dry-run toggle unchecked for actual import
Actual import executed — status: APPLIED with summary counts
steps.visual_check: Import result showing status: APPLIED, summary with created/updated counts, empty warnings array. Permission configuration re-applied successfully.

journeys.name: Verify Cache Invalidation After Permission Change
journeys.description: Admin modifies an access group's permissions, then the affected user's next request reflects the updated permissions without waiting for cache TTL.

steps.expected_result: Access group list loaded (admin session)
SALES_STAFF detail page loaded

steps.target: canAccess checkbox for system.resources.list (currently unchecked for SALES_STAFF)
steps.expected_result: canAccess now checked for Resource Registry in SALES_STAFF group
canView now checked
Permissions saved — cache invalidated for all SALES_STAFF users
steps.visual_check: Success toast 'Permissions updated' — permission cache for sales user should be invalidated

steps.expected_result: Navigate to login to switch users
Login form populated
Login succeeds with updated permissions
steps.visual_check: Dashboard sidebar now shows 'Resource Registry' link for sales user — the permission change took effect immediately (cache was invalidated by the accessGroup.updated event)

steps.expected_result: Resource Registry page accessible — confirming cache invalidation worked
steps.visual_check: Resource Registry page loads successfully for the sales user — previously denied, now allowed after SALES_STAFF permission update

steps.expected_result: Switch back to admin to revert change
Admin login
Admin logged in
List loaded
Detail page
Reverted to original state
Reverted
SALES_STAFF permissions restored to original
